steps.title: Assign quantities to each pan size
steps.content:
From the problem:
  • 8-inch pans: $n$ (the defined variable)
  • 12-inch pans: $5n$ (five times the number of 8-inch pans)
  • 6-inch pans: $13$ (given directly)

steps.title: Write the total equation
steps.content:
All three quantities must sum to 121:
$$5n + n + 13 = 121$$
Combine like terms:
$$6n + 13 = 121$$

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: $12\left(5n\right)+8n+6\left(13\right)=121$ — This equation multiplies the pan quantities by the pan sizes (12, 8, 6 inches), which gives total inches rather than total count. The problem asks for a count equation. Eliminated.
  • B: $12n+8n+6n=121$ — This treats every pan size as $n$ pans, ignoring that 6-inch pans $= 13$ and 12-inch pans $= 5n$. Eliminated.
  • C: $5n+13=121$ — This omits the 8-inch pans ($n$). Eliminated.
  • D: $6n+13=121$ — This correctly combines $5n + n = 6n$ (12-inch plus 8-inch pans) and adds the 13 fixed 6-inch pans. That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
